name: C5 — Cloud Computing Compliance Criteria Catalogue
owner: Germany Federal Office for Information Security (BSI)
description: BSI's Cloud Computing Compliance Criteria Catalogue (C5) — baseline security criteria for cloud service providers, used as the basis for independent C5 attestation audits performed under ISAE 3000 (structurally comparable to SOC 2 / ISO 27001). C5:2020 defines 121 criteria across 17 domains; C5:2026 defines 168 criteria across 18 domains. A separate General Conditions series (BC-01…BC-06 in 2020) became the audited GC domain in 2026.
relationToCloudSecurity: Core cloud-security assurance catalogue; the de facto standard for cloud security attestation in Germany and widely referenced across the DACH region.
relationToAISecurity: Complemented by AIC4, which extends C5-style attestation to AI-based cloud services.

license.notes: Government publication; free download with BSI copyright retained. Generally permitted for reference and citation; consult BSI's terms before redistribution at scale.

notes: Enriched from auto-generated bulk metadata. Reclassified from regulation to control (C5 is an attestation criteria catalogue, not legislation).
